parameters.design: 2 methods x 3 selections x 2 budgets + 2 recent controls

prediction_outcome: No scenario predicted K-norm/true-H2O anti-correlation. Scenario B closest for 33% budget (all methods equal under masking). Budget-dependent reversal at 50% was unpredicted.
key_finding: K-norm and cumulative attention are ANTI-CORRELATED on Llama (rho=-0.431, Jaccard=0.057). True H2O selects WORSE positions than random (Qwen 33.3% vs 91.7%). K-norm captures content-density, cumulative attention captures routing-infrastructure. Most-attended positions are LEAST informative for compression. Budget-dependent reversal at 50%: true_h2o beats k_norm under masking (+14.8pp).
evidence_impact: Added findings #75-80. Added 2 disconfirmed claims. Anti-correlation is novel mechanistic finding separating content-density from routing-frequency in KV positions.
next_planned: Analyze which positions true H2O selects (sink-dominance hypothesis); budget sweep to find crossover point; layer-specific true H2O

null_space_existence.status: CONFIRMED in K-space (p=0.013). Narrow but real. Qwen-specific.

spatial_structure.status: DECISIVELY DISCONFIRMED for selectivity. HEAD-LEVEL CONCENTRATION decisive on Qwen, weak on Llama.

positional_dissociation.status: Complete. Head x position interaction is encoding-dependent.

geometric_dissociation.status: K > V UNIVERSAL at per-head level: 16/16 heads across 2 models.

head_level_analysis.status: COMPLETE: Two-regime on Qwen (decisive), near-collapse on Llama.

natural_usage.status: ADVANCED: 3 eviction benchmarks complete. K-norm vs true H2O ANTI-CORRELATED (rho=-0.431). True H2O < random at 33%. 'Recent' universally best. K-norm captures content-density, not routing.
